target.title: fix(prompt): route untrusted group prompts outside system prompt [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens group prompt handling by separating trusted configured `GroupSystemPrompt` text from prompt-like group metadata that may come from user-controlled channel context.

## Changes

- Preserve trusted/operator-configured `GroupSystemPrompt` content while normalizing real newlines.
- Add `untrustedGroupSystemPrompt` for group prompt-like metadata that must not enter the system prompt.
- Sanitize untrusted group prompt markers and route that text through `UntrustedStructuredContext`.
- Add regression coverage for trusted prompt preservation, untrusted routing, sanitized marker text, and context merging.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/inbound.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean after addressing two actionable findings)

Behavior addressed: Group prompt-like metadata from untrusted channel context is no longer placed in `GroupSystemPrompt`; trusted configured prompts still work without rewriting operator-authored markers.
Real environment tested: Local `/app` checkout on branch `655`, rebased onto `upstream/main` at `49d605ece7`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the validation commands listed above after the final rebase.
Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest reported 2 files passed / 71 tests passed; `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:core:test` exited 0; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: Trusted prompts preserve `[Assistant]` and `System:` text, while `untrustedGroupSystemPrompt` is sanitized to untrusted marker text and rendered as structured untrusted context.
What was not tested: No live channel integration was run; this is covered with focused unit/type proof for the shared inbound context path.

## Notes

- AI-assisted change.
- This tracks the hardening item from NVIDIA-dev/openclaw-tracking#655.
- `USER.md` was updated locally with the requested worklog and intentionally not committed.
